name: POLICY-BACKLOG-13
title: Housekeeping wave 1: Read-before-Edit principle + test consolidation + metadata gaps
description:
Bundled housekeeping items for policy feature, surfaced by wave-1 review.

(M1) HIGH-SIGNAL: Add a general Read-before-Edit principle to coding-rules.md. BUG-6 shipped Inv 35 to rabbit-feature mandating Read-before-Edit for rabbit-feature-spec specifically. The lesson is general: every actor editing existing code should Read it first. Encoding in coding-rules.md propagates via policy-block injection to every subagent dispatch, instead of needing per-skill invariants.

Suggested addition (coding-rules.md Section 1 or as new principle): 'Before editing an existing file, Read it. Before writing alongside existing code, Read the surrounding module. Edits made without reading are speculative.' Wire one assertion in test-rule-files-content.py.

(M11) Rename test-policy-invariants-v1-4-0.py to test-spec-invariants.py (or test-policy-invariants.py). The versioned filename triggers a rename cycle every spec bump (v1-2-0 -> v1-3-0 -> v1-4-0 per run.py comments). Tests inside are not version-specific.

(M12) Delete or consolidate: test-files-exist.py is fully subsumed by test-policy-invariants-v1-4-0.py.

(M13) Policy test scripts missing required Version/Owner/Deprecation criterion module docstring per spec-rules section 3. Non-compliant: test-files-exist.py, test-rule-files-content.py, test-imports-resolve.py, test-backlog003.py, test-policy-invariants-v1-4-0.py, test-backlog-11-12.py, run.py. The policy feature itself violates its own metadata rule.

(LOW F5) test-policy-bug-fixes.py is an unbounded kitchen-sink (9+ closed-ticket assertions). Restate as 'regression guard for historical fixes' with concrete retirement criterion.

(LOW F6) Test filename conventions inconsistent (5 styles across 8 files). Pick one (behavior-first names) and rename.

Acceptance: all policy tests have required metadata; coding-rules.md sanctions Read-before-Edit; versioned filename retired; duplicates consolidated; one test naming convention applied.
